PRIVATE ORBITAL LAUNCH · [ LAUNCH_SITE ] Mass to orbit. Every gram a customer hands us leaves the ground exactly once. AETHON exists to make that moment certain — a two-stage vehicle, a clean insertion, a payload precisely where it was promised. Open the manifest Meet Meridian VEHICLE Meridian STATUS Integrated PAD Standby FIRST FLIGHT [ FIRST_FLIGHT ] date SCROLL 00 THE THRESHOLD There is a silence before ignition. The vehicle is fuelled. The payload is mated. The count holds at nine. For a few seconds, nothing anyone says matters — only what was built. AETHON is built for those seconds. We are not building spectacle. We are building arrival — the right mass in the right orbit at the right minute, with margin held in reserve. Every system on Meridian exists to shorten the distance between a customer’s intention and its place in the sky. 01 THE VEHICLE Meridian Two stages. A booster that comes home. PRIVATE ORBITAL LAUNCH · [ LAUNCH_SITE ] Mass to orbit. Every gram a customer hands us leaves the ground exactly once. AETHON exists to make that moment certain — a two-stage vehicle, a clean insertion, a payload precisely where it was promised. Open the manifest Meet Meridian VEHICLE Meridian STATUS Integrated PAD Standby FIRST FLIGHT [ FIRST_FLIGHT ] date SCROLL

00 THE THRESHOLD There is a silence before ignition. The vehicle is fuelled. The payload is mated. The count holds at nine. For a few seconds, nothing anyone says matters — only what was built. AETHON is built for those seconds. We are not building spectacle. We are building arrival — the right mass in the right orbit at the right minute, with margin held in reserve. Every system on Meridian exists to shorten the distance between a customer’s intention and its place in the sky.

01 THE VEHICLE Meridian Two stages. A booster that comes home. One engine family, flown from sea level to vacuum. Meridian is a two-stage orbital launch vehicle. Its first stage is designed to fly, return, and fly again. Its second stage is built around a single restartable engine — precise enough to set a payload in its slot and walk away. Both stages burn the same propellant, lit by the same engine family: Ardent. VEHICLE HEIGHT [ VEHICLE_HEIGHT ] M MERIDIAN [SN] ELEVATION Vehicle height [ VEHICLE_HEIGHT ] m 05 PAYLOAD 04 STAGE TWO 03 STAGING 02 STAGE ONE 01 PROPULSION 01 PROPULSION Nine Ardent engines A sea-level cluster, deep-throttle capable. Eight hold the ring; one gimbals at the centre for control through ascent and landing. LIFTOFF THRUST [ LIFTOFF_THRUST ] kN 02 STAGE ONE The booster that returns After separation the first stage reorients, relights, and flies a controlled descent to be recovered and reflown. Reuse is the architecture, not an afterthought. 03 STAGING Clean sep

Reuse is the architecture, not an afterthought. 03 STAGING Clean separation Pneumatic pushers and a vented interstage part the stages without pyrotechnic shock reaching the payload. 04 STAGE TWO One engine, vacuum-tuned A single restartable Ardent Vacuum carries the stack to orbit, then relights to circularise, phase, and deorbit itself — leaving nothing behind. 05 PAYLOAD Encapsulated clean Your spacecraft rides inside a composite fairing, encapsulated under cleanroom conditions and mated to the vehicle vertically. 01 PROPULSION Nine Ardent engines A sea-level cluster, deep-throttle capable. Eight hold the ring; one gimbals at the centre for control through ascent and landing. LIFTOFF THRUST [ LIFTOFF_THRUST ] kN
02 STAGE ONE The booster that returns After separation the first stage reorients, relights, and flies a controlled descent to be recovered and reflown. Reuse is the architecture, not an afterthought.
03 STAGING Clean separation Pneumatic pushers and a vented interstage part the stages without pyrotechnic shock reaching the payload.
04 STAGE TWO One engine, vacuum-tuned A single restartable Ardent Vacuum carries the stack to orbit, then relights to circularise, phase, and deorbit itself — leaving nothing behind.
05 PAYLOAD Encapsulated clean Your spacecraft rides inside a composite fairing, encapsulated under cleanroom conditions and mated to the vehicle vertically. STATIC ENVELOPE [ FAIRING_VOLUME ] m³

02 SPECIFICATION The envelope Performance figures are published design targets, confirmed at qualification. Values shown as tokens are pending release. [ PENDING ] CONFIRMED AT QUALIFICATION Payload to LEO 200 KM · 28.5° [ PAYLOAD_LEO ] kg Payload to SSO 500 KM · POLAR [ PAYLOAD_SSO ] kg Vehicle height [ VEHICLE_HEIGHT ] m Fairing — static envelope [ FAIRING_ENVELOPE ] m Liftoff thrust [ LIFTOFF_THRUST ] kN Architecture Two stage · partial reuse First-stage propulsion Nine × Ardent Upper-stage propulsion One × Ardent Vacuum Booster recovery Propulsive return Launch site [ LAUNCH_SITE ]

03 DESTINATIONS Where Meridian delivers Meridian is tuned for the orbits where most of the world’s payloads actually go — low, fast, and Sun-synchronous. LEO SSO POLAR LEO ≈ 200–1,000 km Low Earth Orbit Constellations, technology demonstration, resupply. The busiest neighbourhood in space — and the one Meridian is built for. SSO ≈ 600–800 km Sun-Synchronous Orbit Earth observation and remote sensing, crossing each point at the same local sun-time. Polar, exacting, unforgiving on insertion accuracy. INC On request Dedicated inclination Your orbit, your inclination, your timing. A dedicated mission places the payload exactly — no rideshare compromise on where you end up. Dedicated The whole vehicle, your orbit. Full control of inclination, altitude, and window. Rideshare Share the ride to a standard orbit on a fixed cadence. Lower cost, set destination.
LEO ≈ 200–1,000 km Low Earth Orbit Constellations, technology demonstration, resupply. The busiest neighbourhood in space — and the one Meridian is built for.
SSO ≈ 600–800 km Sun-Synchronous Orbit Earth observation and remote sensing, crossing each point at the same local sun-time. Polar, exacting, unforgiving on insertion accuracy.
INC On request Dedicated inclination Your orbit, your inclination, your timing. A dedicated mission places the payload exactly — no rideshare compromise on where you end up.

04 OPERATIONS How a mission flies From signed manifest to separation confirmation, a Meridian mission moves through the same disciplined sequence every time. Predictability is the product. 01 Manifest We lock your mass, volume, orbit, and window, and assign a mission manager who stays with you to flight. 02 Integration Your spacecraft is received, inspected, and integrated to the payload adapter in a controlled facility. 03 Encapsulation Sealed inside the fairing under cleanroom conditions, then mated to the vehicle. 04 Rehearsal A full wet dress rehearsal — propellant load, countdown, and hold — before anyone commits to flight. 05 Launch The window opens. The count runs. Nine engines light and the stack leaves the pad. 06 Insertion Stage two sets you in your slot, confirms the state vector, and deploys. You have telemetry before the booster is home.

05 PROGRAM The road to first flight AETHON has not yet flown. We say that plainly. Below is the qualification campaign standing between Meridian and orbit — and we publish each gate the moment it clears. G1 Ardent engine qualification Full-duration and restart firing across the flight envelope. STATUS [ STATUS ] G2 Stage test campaign Structural proof, tank cycling, and stage-level hot fire. STATUS [ STATUS ] G3 Integrated vehicle test Full stack on the pad — fuelled, counted down, held. STATUS [ STATUS ] G4 Wet dress rehearsal End-to-end countdown to T-minus seconds, propellant aboard. STATUS [ STATUS ] G5 First orbital flight Maiden flight of Meridian to low Earth orbit. T-0 [ FIRST_FLIGHT ] date

06 MANIFEST Open the manifest If you have mass that needs to be in orbit, start here. Tell us what you’re flying and where it has to go. Every inquiry reaches a launch engineer — not an auto-reply. manifest@aethon.example ORGANISATION* CONTACT NAME* EMAIL* APPROX. PAYLOAD MASS TARGET ORBIT* Select… Low Earth Orbit (LEO) Sun-Synchronous (SSO) Dedicated inclination Not sure yet DESIRED WINDOW THE MISSION Prepare inquiry No commitment. We respond to scope and feasibility before anything else.

The pad is quiet. The vehicle is ready. We are listening for go. AETHON Mass to orbit.
